append_items.title: Evaluate code-intelligence procedures on deterministic local fixtures
append_items.mission: Exercise codebase-structural-map and change-impact-review on deterministic synthetic repository fixtures covering no-engine selection, stale indexes, parser facts versus heuristic and LLM-generated edges, dynamic/configuration relationships, worktree output boundaries, secret and private-memory exclusions, false-positive graph evidence, monorepo impact, PR-comment authorization, and token/cost tradeoffs. Record comparative utility and retain the skills outside core unless controlled model and repository evidence justifies promotion.
acceptance_tests: use synthetic local fixtures and no live repository indexing
do not execute installers, binaries, MCP servers, model providers, hooks, watchers, dashboards, or CI comments
compare ordinary source-search review with graph-assisted review on the same fixture evidence
prove that stale, excluded, heuristic, semantic, and LLM-generated graph evidence cannot become a finding without source verification
exercise dynamic import, configuration-driven routing, generated-code, and worktree-boundary cases
verify secrets, private memory, caches, dependencies, generated outputs, and unrelated repositories remain excluded
record score and transparent token or context-cost estimates
leave core promotion blocked pending controlled model and real-repository evaluation
